top_candidates.reasoning: v3.1's explicit First/Second/Third structure carries all three distinctive prohibitions of 2.47 — right-restricted-to-action ('your jurisdiction extends only to the action itself ... fruits ... are never your jurisdiction'), do-not-claim-authorship-of-fruits ('do not regard yourself as the cause of those fruits'), and reject-attachment-to-inaction ('do not let this become a license for retreat ... inaction disguised as caution'). The quotable line 'Your right is to the deploy, not to the launch metrics' is a near-verbatim engineering paraphrase of karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana. This three-prohibition structure appears only in BG 2.47.

2.48 (samatvaṁ yoga ucyate) develops the same teaching but emphasizes equanimity-in-success-and-failure rather than the right-vs-fruits-vs-inaction triad; the engineering layer's emphasis on 'jurisdiction' rather than 'equanimity' makes this less likely.

3.19 (tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samācara) urges unattached continuous action and reaches similar territory, but lacks the explicit no-claim-on-fruits and reject-inaction triad that the engineering layer reproduces.
